title: Nursery Rhyme. CCCXXXIX. Games.
content:
[An exercise during which the fingers of the child are enumerated.]

        Thumbikin, Thumbikin, broke the barn,
        Pinnikin, Pinnikin, stole the corn.
        Long back'd Gray
        Carried it away.
        Old Mid-man sat and saw,
        But Peesy-weesy paid for a'.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Unknown, titled "Nursery Rhyme. CCCXXXIX. Games.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
